title: Today's Markets Brief: European defense stocks cooling off, Softbank on the rise
summary: European defense stocks consolidating after a boom, Softbank hits record high on AI excitement
imageAlt: Image from source article for European defense stocks are cooling off after the military spending boom. Here's what's next
imageCaption: European defense stocks are cooling off after the military spending boom. Here's what's next
imageSourceTitle: European defense stocks are cooling off after the military spending boom. Here's what's next

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the market headlines. European defense stocks are cooling off after a significant boom, driven by increased military spending. According to CNBC, analysts see 2026 as a period of consolidation for the sector, with excitement over Europe's defense budget giving way to company-specific drivers.

One stock that's not cooling off is Softbank, which has hit a record high on the back of the AI boom. The company has overtaken Toyota as Japan's biggest company, which is quite a feat. I mean, Toyota is a household name - it's like the Apple of Japan, but with better fuel efficiency.

The defense stocks consolidation is interesting, as it highlights the shifting landscape of European defense spending. Meanwhile, Softbank's rise is a testament to the market's enthusiasm for AI-related growth. It's like the whole world is suddenly obsessed with AI - I mean, who isn't, right?

My read is that we're seeing a bit of a rotation in the market, with investors moving away from defense stocks and towards AI-related plays. Softbank's surge is a prime example of this trend. As for European defense stocks, it seems like the party's over for now - but I'm sure there'll be another boom on the horizon. After all, as the saying goes, 'the only constant in the market is change'.
